intro: Argentina's rental market operates under specific regulations (DNU 70/2023), inflation indices (BCRA's ICL), real estate commissions, and recurring expenses like [condo fees](/apartment-condo-fees-m2). This calculator compares quarterly, 4-monthly, and semi-annual rent adjustments based on [actual inflation](/argentina-inflation). All figures are current for 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **Rent × (1+i)^periods**. Direct application with your inputs. Compare quarterly, 4-monthly, and semi-annual adjustments based on real inflation rates.

explanation:
Argentina's **rental market** is regulated by the Civil and Commercial Code plus DNU 70/2023 (which partially repealed Law 27.551). Parties have freedom to negotiate currency, term length, and adjustment indices.

## How it's calculated

This calculator compares rental increases with quarterly, 4-monthly, and semi-annual adjustment frequencies using actual inflation rates.

The tool applies the formula with your inputs, checking typical ranges and alerting for unusual values.

## Important notes

This calculation is for reference purposes. For critical decisions (financial, legal, real estate), consult with a specialized professional. Figures are current through 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How often can landlords adjust rent in Argentina?
faq.a: Under DNU 70/2023, parties can freely negotiate adjustment frequency. Options include quarterly (every 3 months), 4-monthly (every 4 months), semi-annual (every 6 months), or annual adjustments.

faq.q: Should I prefer quarterly or annual adjustments as a tenant?
faq.a: With high inflation, more frequent adjustments benefit landlords. Annual adjustments are more tenant-friendly. It's negotiable at lease signing.

faq.q: What inflation indices can be used in Argentina?
faq.a: Common indices include the BCRA's ICL (Índice de Costo de Vida), IPC (Consumer Price Index), UVA (Unidad de Valor Adquisitivo), or USD. The choice is negotiable between parties.
No. Adjustments apply starting the next adjustment period, not retroactively to earlier months.

faq.q: What documents do landlords typically require?
faq.a: Landlords may request a property guarantee (owner-guarantor), surety insurance (insurer coverage), pay stubs showing rent is 3x your monthly salary, or joint guarantees.

faq.q: Can I break a lease early if rent increases?
faq.a: Yes, with notice and penalties. After the first year: 1 month penalty. After that: 1.5 months penalty. The law allows 3 months written notice to terminate.

faq.q: Is there a cap on annual rent increases?
faq.a: Under DNU 70/2023, adjustment terms are freely negotiated. Without DNU restrictions, annual increases follow the BCRA's ICL. All terms are negotiable at lease signing.
